versions.content: Identity is a card type. A player's identity card represents the character they are playing in the game. An identity card is double-sided, with a hero form on one side and an alter-ego form on the other.

term: In Play and Out of Play

versions.content: The playing area, which includes all heroes, alter-egos, allies, minions, villains, attachments, side schemes, the main scheme, and environments, is considered 'in play'. Cards in a player's hand, deck, or discard pile, and cards in the encounter deck or discard pile are 'out of play'. Abilities can only be triggered from cards in play unless specified otherwise.
Incite is a keyword. When a card with the incite X keyword is revealed, place X threat on the main scheme.

An interrupt is a triggered ability that is triggered immediately before its specified triggering condition occurs. The resolution of an interrupt ability precedes the resolution of its triggering condition.
